title: Open To Me

sections.label: Intro
lines: (Ah, ah, ah...)

sections.label: Verse 1
lines: I heard your voice upon my door,
drenched in midnight’s silver glow.
“My love, my dove, open for me,”
your whispers traced my very soul.

sections.label: Pre-Chorus
lines: The night was cold, yet love ran wild,
your fragrance lingered on the air.
But when I rose, you slipped away—
like a shadow lost in prayer.

sections.label: Chorus
lines: Oh, where have you gone, my heart’s desire?
I run through the streets with a soul on fire.
The watchmen gaze, the stars look down,
but only love can lead me now.

sections.label: Verse 2
lines: My hands still drip with myrrh divine,
from the lock where you once stood near.
Every touch still burns in time,
like whispers only heaven hears.

sections.label: Pre-Chorus
lines: I searched for you in empty halls,
I called your name to restless winds.
The dawn arose, but love remained,
a longing that will never end.

sections.label: Chorus
lines: Oh, where have you gone, my heart’s desire?
I run through the streets with a soul on fire.
The watchmen gaze, the stars look down,
but only love can lead me now.

sections.label: Bridge
lines: If you find him, tell my King,
this heart is his, this love still sings.
No earthly joy, no silver gleams,
can quench the fire in my dreams.

sections.label: Final Chorus
lines: Oh, where have you gone, my heart’s desire?
I run through the streets with a soul on fire.
The watchmen gaze, the stars look down,
but only love can lead me now.

sections.label: Outro
lines: (Ah, ah, ah…)

epigraph: it is the voice of my beloved that knocketh
lyricEyebrow: From the Book of Song of Songs
